g.text: Learn to organize and reuse code effectively

g.data-narration: Methods are reusable blocks of code that perform specific tasks. They help organize programs and avoid repeating code.
g.text: A method is a block of code that performs a specific task and can be called
multiple times from different parts of a program.

g.data-narration: Methods provide three key benefits: code reusability, code organization, and encapsulation of data and behavior.
Every method has a structure with access modifier, non-access modifier, return type, name, and parameters.
Access modifiers control visibility: public allows all access, private restricts to same class, protected allows subclasses.
Non-access modifiers add behavior info: static for class methods, final prevents overriding, abstract requires subclass implementation.
g.text: static - Belongs to class, not instance

g.data-narration: Return types specify what value a method returns. Use void when no value is returned, or a data type for returning values.
g.text: public int add(int a, int b) { return a + b; }

g.data-narration: Method names should be descriptive and follow Java naming conventions. Parameters are input variables passed to the method.
g.text: Parameters: Variables passed to method (e.g., int width, int height)

g.data-narration: Void methods perform tasks without returning values. They use System.out to display output.
Value-returning methods use the return keyword to send back a computed result to the caller.
g.text: public int add(int a, int b) {

g.data-narration: Methods are called using objectName.methodName(). Static methods use ClassName.methodName().
Java uses pass-by-value: primitives pass copies (unchanged), objects pass references (can be modified).
Methods provide three main benefits: reusing code without duplication, organizing code logically, and encapsulating behavior securely.
For exams, remember to identify method components, understand modifiers, and know the difference between pass-by-value and reference.
g.text: • Identify and explain method components (access modifiers, return types, parameters)
• Write simple methods with parameters and return values

g.data-narration: Methods organize code into reusable blocks with access controls, return types, and parameters. They enable better code design.
g.text: • void = no return; use return for values
